summary: `import` scans `<harness>/specs/*.md` into `<into>/specs/<name>/` (meta.toml + SPEC.md) and adds `[[spec]]` roll-up rows alongside skills and rules.

per.section: Worked example: `spec`, temper's own custom kind

edit.description: Add `discover_spec_files` (immediate `<harness>/specs/*.md`), `import_spec` (write `<into>/specs/<name>/{meta.toml, SPEC.md}` byte-faithful), thread a third `specs` vec through `run`, and extend `write_rollup` with `doc["spec"] = rollup_tables(specs)`. Add an `ImportError::Spec(#[from] SpecError)` variant.

tests.asserts: a harness carrying `specs/*.md` yields per-spec surface dirs and name-sorted `[[spec]]` roll-up rows; re-import is byte-identical (idempotent); a skill/rule-only harness emits no `[[spec]]` bytes.

acceptance: Importing a harness with `specs/*.md` writes a `specs/<name>/` surface per spec plus `[[spec]]` roll-up rows; re-import produces no diff.
notes: SPEC-KIND-IR shipped (7befdf9; `src/spec.rs` + `pub mod spec` on disk), so this is now `open`. An empty `ArrayOfTables` emits nothing (the existing `[[rule]]` behavior), so the `acceptance__coordinate_import_surface` snapshot and `tests/cli.rs` over skill-only fixtures stay byte-stable — no integration-test blast radius.

summary: `Workspace` carries `specs`, loaded from `<dir>/specs/*` via `Spec::from_surface_dir`, name-sorted beside skills and rules.

per.section: Worked example: `spec`, temper's own custom kind

edit.description: Add `specs: Vec<Spec>` to `Workspace`, load it from `<dir>/specs/*` in `Workspace::load` via the existing `surface_dirs` helper, add a `WorkspaceError::Spec(#[from] SpecError)` variant, and fix the one `Workspace { .. }` literal (the test at src/check.rs:399) to carry `specs: Vec::new()`.

tests.asserts: `Workspace::load` reconstructs spec artifacts from a written specs surface, name-sorted, alongside skills and rules; a workspace with no `specs/` dir loads an empty spec set.

acceptance: `Workspace::load` reconstructs the spec artifacts from a written specs surface; existing skill/rule load tests stay green.
notes: SPEC-KIND-IR shipped, so this is now `open`. The only `Workspace { .. }` literal is `src/check.rs:399` (its own test); `tests/acceptance.rs` uses `Workspace::load`, so the blast radius is check.rs alone. Disjoint from the import.rs edit.

summary: Dispatch `check` over specs: a `spec_features` extractor + embed `contracts/spec.toml`, running admissibility and `engine::validate` (the advisory `max_lines` clause) over the spec features.

per.section: Worked example: `spec`, temper's own custom kind

gate.reason: the untracked `contracts/spec.toml` is curated human territory (build embeds via `include_str!` but never writes/adds it); a human must commit it before the gate can embed it cleanly

edit.description: Add `spec_features(&Spec) -> Features` mirroring `rule_features` (id = name, `body_lines`, `headings`, `source_dir`; no fields/companions).

Add `BUILTIN_SPEC_CONTRACT = include_str!("../contracts/spec.toml")`; in the `Check` arm project `ws.specs` to spec features, parse the spec floor, run `engine::admissibility` + `engine::validate` over them, and merge into the diagnostic stream.

tests.asserts: `spec_features` exposes the spec id, body line count, and ATX headings; the `max_lines` clause fires (advisory) on an over-length spec and stays silent within budget.

acceptance: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — flagging the over-length specs (00, 10) at advisory severity and exiting 0 (non-blocking) absent `--deny-advisories`.
notes: Build-order: also requires SPEC-KIND-WORKSPACE (for `Workspace.specs`); `spec_features` is added here. Runs `contracts/spec.toml`'s active `max_lines` clause only — its two commented clauses (decisions-name-alternatives, references-resolve) await separate predicates (see `(decision-marker-predicate)` and the references-resolve frontier).
